slides.title: JSON Schema Draft Status, issues and next work

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: :turtle:

chat.author: Austin Wright
chat.text: (majority of hands go up)

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: Nobody has said "I-JSON" yet, right?

chat.author: Paul Hoffman
chat.text: It has been said on the list, yes.

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: Yeah that was Austin :-)

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: (I meant, today.)

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: What as just said in the room?

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: Its never too early to start thinking of security considerations

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: re annotations: if a homoiconic language get self-referential, the fun starts :-)

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: Indeed!

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: My brain produces "JSON representation of a result infoset", for which there is no requirement (but maybe a desire) to somehow look like the JSON input.

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: "reserved location" means that it has some sort of semantics but doesn't actively participate in evaluation/validation

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: What is the meaning of "location" here?

chat.author: Austin Wright
chat.text: s/$id/$schema/

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: "location" -> more or less means keyword, but a particularly inert type of keyword.  "$defs", "$comment", and (perhaps) "default" ar the location keywords

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: There is a keyword taxonomy in the draft

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: Thanks, will look

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: Some of that taxonomy will hopefully get more clear with the result infoset revisions, if folks go for that direction.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: was not aware of geofence schemas, is there a collection of examples we have assembled to test metaschema iterations against?

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: ahh, paul's comment clarifies

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: JSON text == A byte sequence including the specific syntax

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: JSON Value = information model ? JSON Text  = data model / bytestream ?

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: JSON Value = data model.

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: Yes (in my view)

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: There is no information model in sight.

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: I just want to steal the terminology from JSONPath

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: Good :-)

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: Yes to what Austin just said about JSON Value

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: Having JSON Schema look at spaces, trailing zeroes, etc, would be a dramatic expansion of its scope

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: is it that "000" and "0" are different JSON text but the same JSON value?

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: yes

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: ^ thats helpful

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: 000 is not JSON :-), but yes, 1.000 == 1.0

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: Annotations may "attach" to the input instance without changing that (possibly via a "location" as defined in RFC 9535)

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: Yeah, the result infoset does that "attaching"

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: Oh yeah, and understanding annotations as currently written is definitely hard to understand, but re-framing with result infosets is clarifying that for me.  I think that plus what the other commentor said about building things up would help a lot.

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: (that was not what I forgot, though)

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: Put your numbers in a string if you want to do that (seriously, we tell that to people all the time in OpenAPI)

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: LOLOL

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: We do something analogous with OpenAPI examples.

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: This is why getting extensibility right is so important- otherwise the request list is endless

chat.author: Henry Andrews
chat.text: Thanks Paul, Lisa, Andy, Austin, everyone who spoke!
